title: Chemically Reactive Mineral Waste Control Procedure
preamble: This procedure defines requirements for managing chemically reactive mineral wastes — including acid rock drainage (ARD), metalliferous drainage, and other reactive tailings or waste rock — to prevent environmental contamination, protect receiving water bodies, and safeguard human health.
owns: Segregation requirements for reactive vs non-reactive material
references: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0034 – Water Quality (water quality monitoring near reactive waste)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0039 – Waste Control (waste classification and disposal)
referenced_by: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0037 references RTPR-HSE-PRO-0036 for reactive waste landform rehabilitation design

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – Materials & Waste Standard]
requirements.text: Require geochemical characterisation of all waste rock and tailings to identify reactive materials using Acid-Base Accounting (ABA) and kinetic testing where required. Require classification of all waste material into reactive and non-reactive streams before disposal decisions are made.
Conduct a comprehensive waste characterisation programme to identify potentially hazardous materials and set the basis for waste management design.

requirements.tag: [TSM – TSM – Tailings Management Protocol, Indicator TM2]
requirements.text: Implement site-specific tailings management systems that address geochemical characterisation of tailings and ARD potential as a core component of the tailings facility management plan.

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – Materials & Waste Standard]
requirements.text: Require segregation of reactive and non-reactive waste materials. Define encapsulation, co-disposal, or neutralisation requirements for reactive materials based on the geochemical characterisation. Document segregation plans and disposal locations in the waste register.
Design waste containment facilities to prevent seepage reaching sensitive environmental receptors, and implement engineering controls (covers, liners, water diversion) appropriate to the waste class.

requirements.tag: [ICMM – ICMM – Position Statement on Mining and Protected Areas / Tailings]
requirements.text: Manage chemically reactive mineral waste in accordance with the Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management (GISTM) where it applies to the specific tailings facility.

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – Water Management Standard]
requirements.text: Define monitoring requirements for leachate, seepage, and receiving water quality downstream of reactive waste storage areas. Define trigger thresholds and adaptive management responses for water quality deterioration.

requirements.tag: [TSM – TSM – Tailings Management Protocol, Indicator TM4]
requirements.text: Conduct an annual tailings management review and maintain an Operation, Maintenance and Surveillance (OMS) manual conforming to applicable guidance.

The project must define and document, in consultation with a qualified geochemist, the site-specific water quality triggers for ARD-affected drainage that will initiate emergency response and enhanced controls, and the closure design criteria for reactive waste landforms.

project_completion_guidance: Commission a geochemical characterisation programme for this project's waste rock and tailings before waste disposal decisions are made. The procedure requires this — insert the programme scope and timeline, and reference the results when available.
Define the project site-specific water quality triggers for ARD-affected drainage. These must be set in consultation with a qualified geochemist. Insert the trigger table.
Define the project waste segregation plan: where reactive material is placed; how the placement boundary is defined; how workers are informed of segregation requirements.

[APPC – Appendix C, C-4 §92.3.2]  Require that Works are conducted to minimise overall mineral waste production while maximising recovery of mineral waste types for rehabilitation and closure activities.
[APPC – Appendix C, C-4 §92.3.5]  Require that adverse impacts related to chemically reactive mineral waste are contained within the Works area and limited to the duration of construction.
[APPC – Appendix C, C-4 §92.3.7]  Require active management of water movement across the Works area to prevent contamination through chemically reactive mineral waste. Controls must be in place before waste rock movement commences.
Define the project closure design criteria for reactive waste landforms: what long-term water quality standard must be achieved; what monitoring confirms closure success.

external_sources.clause: RT Environment and Nature – Materials & Waste Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires geochemical characterisation of all waste rock and tailings using ABA and kinetic testing, classification into reactive and non-reactive streams, and segregation with encapsulation, co-disposal, or neutralisation for reactive materials.

external_sources.clause: RT Environment and Nature – Water Management Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires monitoring of leachate, seepage, and receiving water quality downstream of reactive waste storage areas, with defined trigger thresholds and adaptive management responses for water quality deterioration.

Requires a comprehensive waste characterisation programme to identify potentially hazardous materials and design waste containment facilities that prevent seepage reaching sensitive environmental receptors.
Requires site-specific tailings management systems that address geochemical characterisation of tailings and ARD potential as a core component of the tailings facility management plan.
Requires an annual tailings management review and maintenance of an Operation, Maintenance and Surveillance manual conforming to applicable guidance.

external_sources.clause: Position Statement on Mining and Protected Areas / Tailings
external_sources.summary: Requires management of chemically reactive mineral waste in accordance with the Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management where it applies to the specific tailings facility.
